articles.title: Lawmakers May Continue to Inspect ICE Detention Centers, Appeals Court Rules
articles.summary: A federal appeals court declined, for now, to halt an order requiring D.H.S. to permit unannounced visits to immigration detention centers by Democrats in Congress.

articles.title: Organizers of a canceled human rights conference in Zambia say China intervened
articles.summary: Zambia was due to host Rights Con, the world's biggest digital rights gathering, this week -- but abruptly canceled the event. Organizers blame Chinese pressure for the decision.

articles.title: Military drills on the edge: U.S. and allies test capabilities near Asia's flash points
articles.summary: From drone boats to long-range missiles, the U.S. and its allies tested new military tactics in the Philippines, sharpening deterrence as tensions with China intensify.

articles.title: Facing Pressure, Trump Officials Reject Claims They’re Softening on Immigration
articles.summary: Immigration hard-liners have grown frustrated with the level of deportations and the Department of Homeland Security’s attempts, under its new secretary, to stay under the radar.

articles.title: Mahmoud Khalil Hurtles Toward Potential Deportation as U.S. Speeds Case
articles.summary: A key judicial decision in Mr. Khalil’s immigration case was expedited significantly and included the recusal of multiple judges.

articles.title: U.S. Conducts Third Boat Strike in 5 Days, Killing 2 and Leaving a Survivor
articles.summary: Of the 57 attacks in the Caribbean and eastern Pacific against boats accused of drug trafficking, there have rarely been survivors.

articles.title: U.F.O. Files Released by U.S. Shed Light on What the Government Knows
articles.summary: The Pentagon on Friday released online what it called “new, never-before-seen” files, dating back decades, related to unidentified flying objects.

articles.title: Trump Administration Wants to Strip 12 Immigrants of U.S. Citizenship
articles.summary: The targeted Americans are accused of misdeeds that can qualify them to lose citizenship, but denaturalization has rarely been invoked in the past.

articles.title: Trump Gave Out a No-Bid Contract to Turn D.C.’s Reflecting Pool Blue
articles.summary: President Trump handpicked a firm he said had worked on his swimming pool to repair the iconic site near the Lincoln Memorial.

articles.title: Virginia’s Top Court Delivers a Major Victory for Republicans
articles.summary: Also, what health experts want you to know about hantavirus. Here’s the latest at the end of Friday.

articles.title: Russia's Victory Day celebrations are dampened by ongoing war with Ukraine
articles.summary: World War 2 Victory Day celebrations in Moscow will be muted this year because of fears of drone attacks, as the public tires of the Ukraine war.

articles.title: UFO files spanning decades are released by Defense Department
articles.summary: Cold War reports of mysterious rotating saucers; recent sightings of metallic elliptical objects floating in mid-air. Those and other reports of unidentified anomalous phenomena or UAPs — the military

articles.title: Canvas is back online, but questions — and final exam disruptions — linger
articles.summary: Some schools are warning users not to log back into Canvas yet, after a ransomware group claimed credit for a data breach. Half of North America's higher education institutions use the platform.

articles.title: Colombia's rogue hippos could find refuge in India
articles.summary: In Colombia, a plan to cull Pablo Escobar's invasive hippos is challenged by an Indian billionaire's offer to relocate dozens of the animals to India's wildlife reserve instead.

articles.title: Contact tracing could be key in halting the spread of hantavirus. Here's how it works
articles.summary: More than two dozen passengers left the cruise ship before the outbreak was identified. The race is on to connect with them to monitor their health — and the health of those they've interacted with.

articles.title: As federal government pulls back, Colorado charts independent course with vaccines
articles.summary: As state leaders change laws to make vaccines more accessible, a coalition of doctors, public health advocates and everyday Coloradans is trying to start a public conversation about their importance.

articles.title: Court rejects Virginia redistricting in a blow to Democrats' counter to Trump, GOP
articles.summary: Virginia voters approved redistricting that could help Democrats pick up four House seats. Democrats said it was to counter the gains that Trump and the GOP have picked up in Republican-led states.

articles.title: Rooted in nature, 'Silent Friend' will change the way you see the trees
articles.summary: A new art-house drama tells three stories that span the century — and connect to one tree. Silent Friend will open your eyes to the beauty of the natural world.

articles.title: U.S. intercepts Iranian attacks on 3 ships. And, what to know about hantavirus
articles.summary: The U.S. says it intercepted Iranian attacks yesterday targeting three Navy ships. And, what to know about the hantavirus outbreak that started on a cruise ship.

articles.title: The U.S. fires on Iranian tankers trying to evade its blockade amid a Hormuz standoff
articles.summary: U.S. forces fired on two Iranian oil tankers after exchanging fire with Iranian forces in the Strait of Hormuz. And the United Arab Emirates reported another Iranian missile and drone attack.

articles.title: Spain readies for evacuations as a hantavirus-hit cruise ship heads for Canary Islands
articles.summary: Spanish authorities are preparing to receive more than 140 passengers and crew members on board a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship headed for the Canary Islands.

articles.title: David Attenborough celebrates his 100th birthday
articles.summary: He was born before the Great Depression, came of age in WWII, and is still making wildlife documentaries. Brits call David Attenborough a national hero, as he celebrates his 100th birthday.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
